title: Rigetti Computing Reports Fourth Quarter and Full-Year 2025 Financial Results | Rigetti & Co, LLC
why:
- Rigetti achieved 99.9% two-qubit gate fidelity at a 28 nanosecond gate speed on a prototype platform, marking a significant advancement in quantum hardware performance.
- The company received an $8.4 million order from India's Centre for Development of Advanced Computing (C-DAC) for a 108-qubit on-premises superconducting quantum computer based on chiplet architecture, to be deployed in the second half of 2026.
- Rigetti reported median two-qubit gate fidelities of 99.7% on its 9-qubit system, 99.6% on its 36-qubit system, and 99% on its 108-qubit system, underscoring sustained fidelity progress across architectures.
- The company continues to demonstrate chiplet tiling as a scalable approach to building large-scale quantum systems, addressing challenges in manufacturing complexity and fabrication yield.
- Rigetti advanced the deployment and system-level integration of its 108-qubit chiplet-based quantum system, implementing architectural refinements to improve system stability and control.
- Ongoing collaboration with Riverlane is focusing on error correction research and integration, supporting Rigetti's roadmap toward error mitigation and system scalability.
